name: CloudTalk Business VoIP API
what_it_does: Cloud-based business VoIP and call center platform with REST API — providing intelligent call routing, power dialer for outbound sales, call recording and transcription, IVR flows, real-time analytics, and CRM integration for sales and support teams.
use_cases: Managing phone numbers and extensions programmatically via CloudTalk REST API
Pulling call recordings and transcripts for quality assurance workflows
Querying call analytics and agent performance metrics for revenue operations
Integrating CloudTalk call events with CRM via webhook for automatic call logging
Automating contact list uploads for power dialer campaigns via API
not_for: High-volume contact center (use Genesys or NICE for enterprise contact center)
SMS-first communication (use Twilio or MessageBird for SMS)
best_when: A sales or customer support team of 5-200 agents needs cloud VoIP with power dialing, CRM integration, and good analytics at mid-market pricing.
avoid_when: You need a pure enterprise contact center platform with WFM, or your primary channel is SMS/messaging rather than voice.

auth.notes: API credentials from CloudTalk account settings. HTTP Basic auth with API key ID and API key secret. No OAuth2 or scopes. Webhook for call events (start, answer, end, recording). Sub-account API keys supported for resellers.

pricing.notes: Slovak company (Bratislava). Founded 2016. Raised $30M. 2,500+ customers. 160+ countries supported. Power dialer, smart dialer, and predictive dialer options. Native integrations: Salesforce, HubSpot, Pipedrive, Zendesk. Real-time analytics dashboard. Strong European customer base.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits per account tier

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for calls, contacts, agents, numbers, and statistics. Basic auth with ID/secret pair. Good developer documentation. Call recording download via pre-signed URLs. Webhook delivers real-time call events. Contact deduplication logic important — CloudTalk merges by phone number.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Call reads idempotent. Agent reads idempotent. Contact reads idempotent by phone number. Contact creation may create duplicates — check for existing before creating.
known_agent_gotchas: Basic auth uses ID/secret pair, not single API key — both values required
Call recordings are available after call completion with processing delay
Contact matching by phone number — ensure E.164 format for consistent deduplication
Power dialer campaigns require contact list upload before launch
Recording download URLs are time-limited pre-signed URLs

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR and SOC2 compliant. EU and US data residency. Basic auth with credential pair. Call recordings may contain sensitive conversations — implement secure access controls. GDPR call recording consent notices required in EU.
